{"step_by_step_thinking": "Benign intracranial hypertension (BIH), also known as pseudotumor cerebri, is characterized by increased intracranial pressure without an identifiable cause. The main features of BIH include headache, visual disturbances, and papilledema. Proptosis, which is bulging of the eye, is not a feature of BIH. Normal size ventricles, headache, and papilledema are all consistent with the diagnosis of BIH.", "answer_choice": "A"}
